label: Emptiness and Negation
description: Terms that work around negation, void, and the absence of inherent existence. The conceptual ground of Madhyamaka philosophy and Chan practice.

label: Mind and Consciousness
description: Terms for the cognitive-affective organ, its layers, and its movements. Covers both the unified heart-mind (心) and the scholastic taxonomy of consciousness types.

label: Path and Practice
description: Terms for the way walked, the methods undertaken, and the cultivation of practice. Bridges Daoist, Confucian, and Buddhist senses of 'the Way.'

label: Western Philosophy and Logic
description: Meiji coinages for the systematic disciplines of Western philosophy: metaphysics, epistemology, logic, and their methods. Mostly attributable to Nishi Amane.

label: Liberal Political Philosophy
description: Coinages for the vocabulary of Western liberal democratic thought: rights, freedom, democracy, society. Arrived as a conceptual cluster in the 1870s–1880s.

label: Natural Science and Method
description: Coinages for the institutions and methods of Western natural science: systematic inquiry, experiment, hypothesis, disciplinary organization.

label: Culture and Civilization
description: Coinages for the broader social and civilizational vocabulary imported from Western modernity: society, culture, civilization as distinct conceptual spheres.
